name: Bay Mare
description: A sure-footed bay mare with a simple riding saddle, dependable for travel between settlements and inland trails.

name: Gray Gelding
description: A calm gray gelding valued for steady work, road travel, and the long distances of the Atlantic frontier.

name: Chestnut Stallion
description: A spirited chestnut horse with the strength and speed to carry a rider across rough colonial roads.

name: Dappled Trail Horse
description: A dappled riding horse suited to explorers, messengers, and travelers moving beyond established settlements.

name: Pack Horse
description: A saddle-equipped pack horse prepared to carry supplies, maps, and trade goods overland.

name: Surveyor's Mule
description: A hardy mule prized for balance and endurance when carrying instruments across uneven ground.

name: Coastal Pony
description: A compact pony well suited to coastal paths, smaller settlements, and everyday travel along the Atlantic shore.

name: Merchant's Wagon
description: A covered wagon for moving cargo, household goods, and trade supplies between ports, markets, and inland communities.

name: Fishing Dory
description: A sturdy small wooden boat for coastal fishing, short harbor crossings, and work along Atlantic waterways.

name: Atlantic Sloop
description: A small sailing vessel prepared for coastal trade and exploration, carrying travelers between ports of the Atlantic world.
